--- title: "Towards translational and mechanistic whole-brain simulations with The Virtual Brain" subtitle: "Track 4 Workshop â€” FAIR Brain Data Science Bootcamp" authors: - name: "Leon Stefanovski" - name: "Marius Pille" - name: "Leon Martin" affiliations: - id: 1 name: "CharitĂ© University Medicine Berlin Â· Berlin Institute of Health" date: 2026-05-12 format: clean-revealjs: slide-number: true scrollable: true auto-play-media: false chalkboard: true incremental: false toc: false logo: img/Charite.png footer: "Â© Pille, Martin, Stefanovski, CharitĂ© Unviersity Medicine Berlin" link-external-newwindow: true code-line-numbers: true highlight-style: github include-in-header: _slides-home-btn.html engine: jupyter jupyter: python3 --- ## Welcome {.unnumbered} - Two-day hands-on workshop on personalized whole-brain modeling with TVB - Day 1: structured sessions - Day 2: mini hackathon â€” bring your own questions / data - Useful links: - - - ## Schedule {.unnumbered} ::: {.columns} ::: {.column width="60%" .smaller} **Day 1**\ **Block 1 - 09:00-12:00:**\ 1. Intro to Brain Network Modelling\ 2. Defining a FAIR brain network model\ 3. Dynamical perspective\ 4. Setup & First examples **Block 2 - 13:00-15:00:**\ 5. Bifurcation Theory\ 6. Intro to The Virtual Brain {background-color="#0b3d91"} - TODO: motivation for whole-brain network modeling - TODO: brief historical arc of TVB ## Basic ideas and history :::: {.columns} ::: {.column width="25%"} ![](img/figures/basic_idea_imaging_workflow1.png){width="100%"} ::: ::: {.column width="25%" .fragment} ![](img/figures/basic_idea_imaging_workflow2.png){width="100%"} ::: ::: {.column width="25%" .fragment} ![](img/figures/basic_idea_imaging_workflow3.png){width="100%"} ::: ::: {.column width="25%" .fragment} ![](img/figures/basic_idea_imaging_workflow4.png){width="100%"} ::: :::: ## Mathematical framework $$ dS_i = \left[f_d(S_i, \theta^d, C_i, I_i) \right]dt + g(S_i, \theta^g)\, dW_i $$ ::: {.fragment} **State Evolution** - $S_i$ - state variables at node $i$ - $f_d$ - dynamics function with parameters $\theta^d$ - $C_i$ - coupling input from connected nodes - $I_i$ - external input (stimulation, driving signals) - $g$ - noise diffusion coefficient with parameters $\theta^g$ - $dW_i$ - Wiener process (stochastic fluctuations) ::: ## Dynamical Systems :::: {.columns} ::: {.column style="font-size: 0.55em;"} A mass $m$ on a spring with stiffness $k$.
Perpetual oscillation at $\omega = \sqrt{k/m}$. **State Equations:** $$ \dot{x} = v $$ $$ \dot{v} = - \frac{k}{m}*x $$ **State Variables** | **Variable** | **Initial Value** | **Unit** | **Description** | |--------------|-------------------|----------|-----------------| | $x$ | 2.0 | $\mathrm{m}$ | Displacement from equilibrium | | $v$ | 0.0 | $\frac{\mathrm{m}}{\mathrm{s}}$ | Velocity | **Parameters** | **Parameter** | **Value** | **Unit** | **Description** | |---------------|-----------|----------|-----------------| | $k$ | 0.0001 | $\frac{\mathrm{N}}{\mathrm{m}}$ | Spring stiffness constant | | $m$ | 1.0 | $\mathrm{kg}$ | Mass of the oscillating body | ::: ::: {.column} ::: {.r-stack} ![](notebooks/1_1_MathematicalFramework/spring_single_thumb.png){fig-align="center"} ![](notebooks/1_1_MathematicalFramework/spring_single.gif){.fragment fig-align="center"} ::: ::: :::: ## Initial Conditions {background-color="#ffffff"} :::: {.columns} ::: {.column width="20%"} ::: {style="font-size: 0.8em;"} $$ \dot{x} = v $$ $$ \dot{v} = - \frac{k}{m}*x $$ ::: ::: ::: {.column width="80%"} ::: {.r-stack} ![](notebooks/1_1_MathematicalFramework/spring_ics_thumb.png){fig-align="center"} ![](notebooks/1_1_MathematicalFramework/spring_ics.gif){.fragment fig-align="center" } ::: ::: :::: ## Phase Space {background-color="#ffffff"} ![](notebooks/1_1_MathematicalFramework/phase_space.png){fig-align="center" height="580px"} ## Parameters â€” Mass {background-color="#ffffff"} ::: {.r-stack} ![](notebooks/1_1_MathematicalFramework/spring_mass_thumb.png){fig-align="center" height="580px"} ![](notebooks/1_1_MathematicalFramework/spring_mass.gif){.fragment fig-align="center" height="580px"} ::: ## Towards Realism â€” Damping & Load {background-color="#ffffff"} ::: {.r-stack} ![](notebooks/1_1_MathematicalFramework/spring_realism_thumb.png){fig-align="center" height="580px"} ![](notebooks/1_1_MathematicalFramework/spring_realism.gif){.fragment fig-align="center" height="580px"} ::: ## Mean field models & Neural Masses - TODO: notebook link - TODO: pick a model, inspect parameters, simulate - TODO: neural mass / mean-field formulation ## Coupled network dynamics - TODO: nodes, coupling, delays, noise - TODO: connectome loading - TODO: global coupling sweep ::: {.fragment fragment-index=2} $$ C_i = f_c^{\text{post}}\Big(\sum_j A_{ij}\, f_c^{\text{pre}}(S_i, S_j(t-\tau_{ij}), \theta^c), S_i, \theta^c\Big) $$ ::: {style="font-size: 0.55em;"} **Coupling** - $f_c^{\text{pre}}$ - pre-aggregation transformation - $A_{ij}$ - structural connectivity weight ($j \to i$) - $\tau_{ij}$ - transmission delay (tract length / conduction speed) - $f_c^{\text{post}}$ - post-aggregation transformation - $\theta^c$ - coupling parameters ::: ::: # 2. Defining a FAIR brain network model {background-color="#0b3d91"} ## Why FAIR? TVB-O in one slide - TODO: ontology-driven model specification [@Martin2025] - TODO: metadata + automated code generation ## Applications in basic research - TODO: representative findings - TODO: links to [@Schirner2023; @Koller2024; @Kashyap2025] ## Clinical applications - TODO: translational use-cases - TODO: virtual epileptic / Alzheimer / stroke patient examples # 3. Dynamical perspective {background-color="#0b3d91"} ## How to analyse a brain system ::: {.columns} ::: {.column width="33%" .smaller} **Time series** â€” *what* the model does Simulate trajectories $x(t)$ for fixed parameters. Direct comparison with EEG / fMRI signals; spectra, FC, FCD. ::: ::: {.column width="33%" .smaller} **Phase plane** â€” *how* it does it Vector field, nullclines and trajectories in state space. Reveals fixed points, basins of attraction and limit cycles **at one parameter value**. ::: ::: {.column width="34%" .smaller} **Bifurcation diagram** â€” *when* it changes Track equilibria and limit cycles **as a parameter varies**. Locates regime shifts, multistability, oscillation onset. ::: ::: ![](img/bifurcation_overview.png){fig-align="center" width="95%"} ::: {.smaller} The three views are **complementary**: time series live at one $(a, x_0)$; the phase plane shows the geometry around them; the bifurcation diagram organises *all* of them into a single map of regimes. We use all three in the rest of this section. ::: ## Linear stability â€” the simplest bifurcation $$ \dot x = a\,x \quad\Rightarrow\quad x(t) = x_0\,e^{a t} $$ ::: {.columns} ::: {.column width="55%"} - $a < 0$ â†’ **stable** (decay); $a > 0$ â†’ **unstable** (growth); $a = 0$ â†’ **bifurcation point** - For $\dot x = f(x)$ near a fixed point $x^\ast$, linearise: $\dot{\delta x} = J\,\delta x$ with $J = \partial f/\partial x|_{x^\ast}$ - **Stability** is read off the spectrum $\sigma(J) = \{\lambda_k\}$: $\max_k \mathrm{Re}\,\lambda_k < 0$ â†’ stable - A **bifurcation** occurs when an eigenvalue **crosses the imaginary axis** as a parameter changes - real $\lambda \to 0$: saddle-node / pitchfork / transcritical - complex pair $\alpha \pm i\omega \to \pm i\omega$: Hopf ::: ::: {.column width="45%"} ![](img/linear_stability.png){width="100%"} ::: ::: ## 2D portraits â€” node, saddle, focus, centre ![](img/phase_portraits_2d.png){fig-align="center" width="75%"} ## Codim-1 normal forms in the brain ![](img/normal_forms.png){fig-align="center" width="95%"} ::: {.columns} ::: {.column width="33%" .smaller} **Saddle-node** â€” $\dot x = a - x^2$. Two equilibria collide â†’ **regime shift**. ::: ::: {.column width="33%" .smaller} **Pitchfork** â€” $\dot x = a x - x^3$. **Symmetry breaking** â†’ two new branches. ::: ::: {.column width="34%" .smaller} **Hopf** â€” $\alpha\pm i\omega$ crosses zero â†’ **limit cycle** of radius $\sqrt a$. ::: ::: . . . These are the **universal local pictures**: by the centre-manifold and normal-form theorems, any smooth system close to such a bifurcation is *locally diffeomorphic* to one of these few canonical equations. A small tilt unfolds the pitchfork into a **hysteresis loop** â€” two saddle-nodes framing a bistable region: the model *remembers its history*. ## Hopf â€” birth of an oscillation ::: {.columns} ::: {.column width="50%"} ![](img/hopf_3d.png){fig-align="center" width="100%"} ::: ::: {.column width="50%"} ![](img/hopf_birth.gif){fig-align="center" width="100%"} ::: ::: ::: {.smaller} Normal form: $\dot z = (a + i\omega)\,z - |z|^2 z$ with $z = x_1 + i x_2$. As $a$ crosses 0 the spiral **inverts** and a stable limit cycle of radius $\sqrt a$ is born â€” exactly the orbit traced in the GIF. ::: ## Numerical continuation in one slide ::: {.columns} ::: {.column width="55%"} - Track $F(x, a) = 0$ along **arclength** $s$, not along $a$ â€” solves $\bigl[F(x,a),\; \langle (x,a) - (x_0,a_0), t_0 \rangle - \Delta s\bigr] = 0$ - **Predictor**: tangent $t_0 \in \ker J_{(x,a)}F$ â†’ **Corrector**: Newton on the augmented system - Walks **through folds** where $\partial F/\partial x$ is singular (the parameter is no longer a function of the state) - Bifurcations detected via **test functions**: $\det J = 0$ (fold), $\mathrm{Re}\,\lambda = 0$ with $\mathrm{Im}\,\lambda \ne 0$ (Hopf) - **Branch switching** at a Hopf restarts along the centre eigenvector â†’ continues the periodic orbit ::: ::: {.column width="45%"} ![](img/continuation_schematic.png){width="100%"} ::: ::: . . . In TVBO, all of this is one YAML and one call: ```python result = exp.run("bifurcationkit.jl") result.continuations["my_cont"].plot_3d(VOI="x1") ``` ## Generic2dOscillator â€” brain-relevant example ![](img/g2d_bifurcation.png){fig-align="center" width="75%"} ## 3.1 Bifurcation analysis â€” hands-on - Notebook: [`notebooks/03_bifurcation_analysis.qmd`](notebooks/03_bifurcation_analysis.qmd) - Same `Continuation` schema, single backend (`bifurcationkit.jl`) - Run on the **Generic2dOscillator** (TVB neural mass): sweep input $I$, continue periodic orbits from each Hopf - **Reading the diagram:** - stable branch â†’ resting / persistent activity - limit cycle â†’ oscillatory regime - fold â†’ abrupt regime shift (e.g. seizure onset) # 4. Parameter exploration and model optimization {background-color="#0b3d91"} ## Brain network models as computational hypotheses - TODO: framing â€” model fitting as hypothesis testing - TODO: TVB-Optim overview [@Pille2025] ## Use-case A â€” Alzheimer's disease - TODO: mechanistic simulation - TODO: integration of biomedical data ## Use-case B â€” homogeneous vs. heterogeneous fitting - TODO: frequency gradients (or another TVBase map) - TODO: comparison protocol # 5. Stimulating the brain {background-color="#0b3d91"} ## Visual and TMS-evoked potentials (Jansenâ€“Rit) - TODO: stimulation primitives in TVB - TODO: VEP / TEP example # Day 2 â€” Mini Hackathon {background-color="#0b3d91"} ## Format - TODO: pitch your question (5 min each) - TODO: form small groups - TODO: checkpoint + wrap-up times ## Bring your ownâ€¦ - TODO: data formats we can support - TODO: EBRAINS / local setup reminder # Wrap-up {background-color="#0b3d91"} ## Take-aways - TODO: what to remember from Day 1 - TODO: where to go next ## Thanks & contact - TODO: contact info - TODO: feedback link | ![](img/Charite.png){fig-alt="Charite logo" width=30%} | ![](img/BIH.png){fig-alt="BIH logo" width=30%} | ![](img/BSS.png){fig-alt="BSS logo" width=30%} | |:---:|:---:|:---:| ## References {.unnumbered} ::: {#refs} ::: ## {.unnumbered}
